jokes.text: You don't see me crying, do you? What do you see me doing, huh? Driving a cab!
jokes.setup: Bobby tries to console someone by saying he doesn't cry about being dropped from a soap opera
jokes.punchline: His 'positive' example is driving a cab - hardly an improvement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's obliviousness - he thinks driving a cab is a positive example of moving on

jokes.text: The last lady you introduced me to kept talking about how all the world's problems could be solved if everybody practiced nude skydiving.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining why he doesn't want Bob to introduce him to another woman
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and ridiculous philosophy of nude skydiving solving world problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with great specificity - 'nude skydiving' is both unexpected and memorable

jokes.text: You realize that, in some primitive cultures, that would mean we're married?
jokes.setup: Alex accidentally hands Joyce something (beer/drink)
jokes.punchline: Joyce's deadpan observation about primitive marriage customs
jokes.explanation: Solid ice-breaker joke that's well-delivered but fairly standard meet-cute humor

jokes.text: Bob leaving abruptly after setting up the introduction
jokes.setup: Bob introduces Alex and Joyce
jokes.punchline: He immediately abandons them in obvious matchmaking fashion
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom matchmaking trope - predictable but serves the story

jokes.text: So, uh... tell me, you cry here often?
jokes.setup: Meeting someone who's crying in a bar
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'do you come here often?' pickup line
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that acknowledges the awkward situation while being charming

jokes.text: That's one good thing about being a cab driver: You never have to worry about getting fired from a good job.
jokes.setup: Joyce mentions getting fired
jokes.punchline: Alex's self-deprecating admission that cab driving isn't a 'good job'
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor that establishes Alex as down-to-earth and self-deprecating

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Congratulations.
jokes.setup: Joyce says she was voted 'most despised woman on daytime TV three years in a row'
jokes.punchline: Alex's sincere congratulations for being despised
jokes.explanation: Nice beat that shows Alex's ability to find the positive in everything

jokes.text: Hey, listen, if I ever have a happy home, you have a standing invitation to come and destroy it anytime you like.
jokes.setup: Joyce was fired for being too old to destroy happy homes
jokes.punchline: Alex offers his future happy home for destruction
jokes.explanation: Charming and clever way to console while also flirting - good character work

jokes.text: I'm not going to let this get me down! At least not until I've had dinner with that swashbuckling ne'er-do-well Alex Rieger.
jokes.setup: Alex giving a motivational speech about not feeling sorry for yourself
jokes.punchline: He describes himself as a 'swashbuckling ne'er-do-well' while asking for a date
jokes.explanation: 'Swashbuckling ne'er-do-well' is wonderfully overwrought language for a cab driver to use about himself

jokes.text: Do you often lose control like that?
jokes.setup: Alex's passionate speech about dinner
jokes.punchline: Joyce's dry observation about his 'loss of control'
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that punctures Alex's dramatic moment

jokes.text: You see those framed documents on the wall? Well, those are subpoenas from the Board of Health.
jokes.setup: Joyce asks why they don't want to eat at Mario's
jokes.punchline: The 'framed documents' are actually health department subpoenas
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag with great misdirection - framing health violations like achievements

jokes.text: Where's your summer home? I open the windows.
jokes.setup: Joyce asks about his summer home after he calls his apartment his 'winter home'
jokes.punchline: Opening windows is his summer home
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and misdirection - sets up expectation of wealth then deflates it perfectly

jokes.text: The vase is from the Ming Dynasty. The Ming dynasty? Yeah. The Ming Dynasty. That's a little shop down on 17th street.
jokes.setup: Alex proudly shows off a vase 'from the Ming Dynasty'
jokes.punchline: The Ming Dynasty is actually a shop name, not the historical dynasty
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with good build-up and payoff - works on multiple levels

jokes.text: You're a man!
jokes.setup: Joyce says there's something she has to tell Alex
jokes.punchline: The obvious fact that he's a man
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing non sequitur that shows Joyce's scattered thinking

jokes.text: Dear, I have company. Don't come out for a while. She stays in there all the time.
jokes.setup: Joyce asks if Alex has been married
jokes.punchline: Alex talks to his 'wife' through a door, then explains she stays in there all the time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - completely unexpected and perfectly delivered deadpan

jokes.text: We didn't shake on it. Right, and I had my fingers crossed all the time.
jokes.setup: Joyce reminds Alex they promised not to get involved
jokes.punchline: Technical loopholes to get out of their agreement
jokes.explanation: Mild callback humor playing on childhood promise-breaking tactics

jokes.text: Lips touching lips! Yuck! What a terrible thought.
jokes.setup: Joyce says kissing will never work
jokes.punchline: Alex's mock disgust at the concept of kissing
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy banter - serviceable but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: Every time you say something funny I want to kiss you. Oh... I wish you didn't tell me that. I'm going to be very self-conscious now because, every time I make a joke, you're going to think I'm just doing it to try to get a... A priest and a rabbi walked into a bar...
jokes.setup: Joyce admits funny comments make her want to kiss Alex, he becomes self-conscious about joke motives
jokes.punchline: He immediately launches into a classic setup while claiming to be self-conscious
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor that acknowledges the mechanism of comedy while using it

jokes.text: Do you mind if we just jump to the punch line?
jokes.setup: Alex starts telling a priest and rabbi joke
jokes.punchline: Joyce's impatient request to skip to the punchline
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-comedy moment that subverts traditional joke structure

jokes.text: I bumped into a chair and told the chair I was sorry.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining signs of his good mood
jokes.punchline: Apologizing to furniture as evidence of happiness
jokes.explanation: Charming character detail that shows Alex's inherent politeness

jokes.text: No, you're in trouble when it goes to the door and scratches to go out.
jokes.setup: Alex worrying about talking to furniture
jokes.punchline: Response about when you're really in trouble - when furniture acts like pets
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that takes the furniture concept to an absurd conclusion

jokes.text: No, Tony. He's just thrilled about being a cab driver. Oh, I get that way myself sometimes.
jokes.setup: Tony asks if Alex's good mood is about Joyce
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion it's about cab driving, Tony's earnest agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing Tony's earnestness against someone's sarcasm

jokes.text: It's like being on a roller coaster and hoping the ride never ends, if you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Alex describing Joyce's emotional intensity
jokes.punchline: Roller coaster metaphor for their relationship
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship metaphor - competent but not particularly original

jokes.text: Louie's entire anti-coffee break speech using George Washington, Abraham Lincoln, and Eisenhower
jokes.setup: Latka taking a coffee break
jokes.punchline: Louie's increasingly ridiculous historical examples of dedication
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Louie - overwrought historical comparisons for coffee breaks

jokes.text: You red pinkie!
jokes.setup: Latka responding to Louie's patriotic guilt trip
jokes.punchline: Mistaken attempt at calling Louie a communist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka malapropism - 'pinkie' instead of 'commie' shows his language confusion

jokes.text: Don't let this go to your head, but you could have your way with me.
jokes.setup: Louie meeting Joyce for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediately inappropriate sexual advance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Louie but predictable - exactly what you'd expect him to say

jokes.text: You're right. I didn't want to meet him.
jokes.setup: Alex warned Joyce she wouldn't want to meet Louie
jokes.punchline: Joyce's immediate confirmation after Louie's crude advance
jokes.explanation: Good callback that provides satisfying confirmation of Alex's warning

jokes.text: I'm going to ask her to marry me. Great idea. What a pro. Perfect. Aw... It's never going to work.
jokes.setup: Friends sympathizing with Alex about Joyce leaving
jokes.punchline: Alex announces marriage plan, friends' supportive responses turn negative
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing how friends flip from supportive to realistic in real time

jokes.text: Proposing? My heavens, no. I'm running for governor, and this is my way of asking you for your vote.
jokes.setup: Joyce realizes Alex is proposing
jokes.punchline: Alex's absurd deflection about running for governor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflection with perfect absurdist logic - completely unexpected response

jokes.text: You've got candles and flowers and a bell. A bell? No, don't! Just forget that bell.
jokes.setup: Joyce listing evidence of Alex's romantic planning
jokes.punchline: Alex's panicked reaction about the bell
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bell as a running gag element - good setup for later payoffs

jokes.text: I'm tired of you. No, I withdraw the proposal.
jokes.setup: Alex claiming two weeks is a long relationship
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal from being tired to withdrawing proposal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's emotional volatility under pressure

jokes.text: you better be quick, because you're not the only girl I'm proposing to tonight.
jokes.setup: Joyce demanding Alex listen to her
jokes.punchline: Alex's claim about multiple proposals in one night
jokes.explanation: Good defensive humor showing Alex's hurt feelings masked by bravado

jokes.text: I have the audacity to believe that I'm good for you.
jokes.setup: Joyce asks what reason he has to want to marry her
jokes.punchline: Alex's self-aware description of his belief as 'audacity'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Alex acknowledges the presumption while standing by it

jokes.text: I told you, not the bell!
jokes.setup: Joyce mentions hearing wedding bells
jokes.punchline: Alex's continued panic about the actual bell
jokes.explanation: Good running gag callback that maintains the absurd bell mystery

jokes.text: They came to pick up my violins. Thanks. Thanks, fellas. Do they have to go back to the shop? Oh, that's terrible. I guess they need a couple of tubes, that's all.
jokes.setup: Bell sound during argument
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be men picking up violins, Alex's matter-of-fact discussion of violin repair
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the bell mystery - completely unexpected explanation that's perfectly logical

jokes.text: We're fighting because we care too much, and we're breaking up because we don't care enough.
jokes.setup: The contradiction in their relationship
jokes.punchline: Alex pointing out the paradoxical logic of their situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about relationship contradictions - perfectly economical

jokes.text: Will you marry me? No! Just checking!
jokes.setup: Alex opens the door to reveal someone inside
jokes.punchline: He immediately proposes to whoever is in there, gets rejected, dismisses it casually
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback combining multiple running gags - the mysterious wife and Alex's proposal obsession

jokes.text: Life is like... an ice cream cone. You got to eat it when you get it. That's right. Got to eat it fast. Because, if you don't, it's going to melt all over your hand and down your arm. That's no good. Do you want to go through life licking your arm?
jokes.setup: Jim giving philosophical life advice
jokes.punchline: Ice cream cone metaphor escalating to the absurd image of licking your arm through life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist philosophy that escalates perfectly to an absurd but vivid conclusion

jokes.text: You should listen to him. He knows what he talking about. Thank you very much. I knew I could help him. Yes, that was very nice what you did for him, Jim. Very nice. Well... Bobby's had it pretty rough. Trying to make it as a boxer, and raise the two kids...
jokes.setup: Jim and Latka thinking they've helped Alex with the life advice
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals he was actually talking about Bobby the whole time
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection reveal - the entire ice cream philosophy was for the wrong person
